titleStmt.title: SII 25.26: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 26. (A.R. No. 26 of 1908.) DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On two stones lying in the court-yard of the Airāvatēśvara temple.
These two pieces of stone are of one and the same record of the king. It contains a belittle portion of the praśasti commencing with Pugaḻśūḻnda-puṇari, etc.
It refers to a transaction of the peruṅguṟi-mahāsabhai of a village (name lost) and to the boundaries of land, the details of which are lost.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
— niṟutti vaṭaticai vākai cūṭittenṟi—Beginning is lost.
⎯⎯ run talaittiṭat tanpoṉ nakarpappuṟattiṭai—
⎯⎯ perun=tiruvum paḻiyukantu koṭutta pukaḻu—
⎯⎯ p paṭuttu maṟṟavar tammai vanacarar tiriyu—
⎯⎯ ṁhāsanattup puvani muḻutuṭaiyāḷo—
⎯⎯ mūrp piṭākai Icaiyāna rājacun=tari nal—
⎯⎯ nayum Ittevar koyilum tirumuṟṟamum—
⎯⎯ viḷaiyātey pāḻkiṭan=tamaiyil Itṉilaṅ—
⎯⎯ ṭuttuk koṇṭuEnd is lost.—

⎯⎯ ṭit tenṟicait temarut—
⎯⎯ tiṭaikiṭappa Innāḷ p piḻai pol niṟaṉ—
⎯⎯ ṇṭalaṅ koḷḷa tiruviḷaittaruḷi veḷḷavaru parittaḷaṅkaḷum—
⎯⎯ lai kaḷiṭṭaruḷi tiṟal koḷ virasiṅhāsa namti—
⎯⎯ timaṅkalattu peruṅkuṟi mahāsabhaiyom—
⎯⎯ kaṇṇāṟṟu 2- ñ catirattu rūpāy 4 Itil teṟkaṭaiya—
⎯⎯ ṟṟu 5 ñā catirattu nilam 4 lil teṟkaṭaiya nilam —
⎯⎯ nilattukku Itu
⎯⎯ ciṟuko
Digital edition of SII 25.26 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
